title: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus do not have evil friends, evil companions, evil associates, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.
content: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus do not have evil friends, evil companions, evil associates, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.

title: The wise, abandoning dark states, Develop bright states, Leaving home for homelessness, In seclusion, hard to delight in. Seek delight in Nibbāna, Abandon sensual pleasures,…
content:
The wise, abandoning dark states,
Develop bright states,
Leaving home for homelessness,
In seclusion, hard to delight in.

Seek delight in Nibbāna,
Abandon sensual pleasures, have nothing,
The wise cleanse themselves
Of defilements from within.

Those with right mind,
Well develop the factors of enlightenment,
Abandon all attachments,
Rejoicing, not clinging,
Without taints, radiant,
They live at peace in the world.

title: Elder Bhūta: When the wise person sees that old age and death are suffering, The ordinary person, not seeing, grasps suffering. Having fully understood suffering,…
content:
Elder Bhūta:

When the wise person sees that old age and death are suffering,
The ordinary person, not seeing, grasps suffering.
Having fully understood suffering, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When craving is destroyed — craving that brings suffering and poison,
Creates proliferation and bondage, pushes toward suffering —
Having destroyed craving, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When with wisdom one sees the supreme good path,
Consisting of eight parts in two groups, purifying all defilements,
Having seen with wisdom, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When one develops the path — sorrowless, stainless,
Unconditioned, peaceful path, purifying all defilements,
Cutting the bonds of fetters, mindful, practicing jhāna,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When thundercloud drums roar in the sky,
Along the bird's path, a dense rain pours down,
A bhikkhu going to a cave, developing, sits in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When on the riverbanks, the forest garlands bloom and shine
With many colors and hues,
With a relaxed mind, sitting in meditation on the riverbank,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When in the dead of night, the forest silent,
A torrential rain pours down,
Tusked beasts are roaring as they live,
A bhikkhu going to a mountainside sits silently in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When applied and sustained thought are subdued,
In a mountain cave, sitting in meditation at ease,
Without fear, without obstruction,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

When sitting with joy and bliss, without defilement, without sorrow,
Unconfined, free from craving, not struck by the dart,
All taints ended, that one sits in meditation,
He finds no happiness superior to this happiness.

title: Possessing these three qualities, bhikkhus, a woman, after death, with the breakup of the body, is mostly reborn in a plane of misery, a bad…
content: Possessing these three qualities, bhikkhus, a woman, after death, with the breakup of the body, is mostly reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell. What three? Here, bhikkhus, a woman, in the morning, lives at home with a mind obsessed by stinginess and miserliness; at noon, lives at home with a mind obsessed by envy and jealousy; in the evening, lives at home with a mind obsessed by sensual desire. Possessing these three qualities, bhikkhus, a woman, after death, with the breakup of the body, is mostly reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell.

title: - There are these four types of meditators, bhikkhus. What are the four? Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled in attainment in concentration but…
content: - There are these four types of meditators, bhikkhus. What are the four? Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled in attainment in concentration but not skilled in duration in concentration. Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled in duration in concentration but not skilled in attainment in concentration. Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled neither in attainment nor in duration in concentration. Here, bhikkhus, a certain meditator is skilled both in attainment and in duration in concentration. Among these four meditators, the one who is skilled both in attainment and in duration in concentration is the foremost, best, chief, highest, and most excellent.

title: If a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma for the purpose of disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called…
content:
If a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma for the purpose of disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called a teacher of Dhamma.
If a bhikkhu practices for the purpose of disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called a bhikkhu who practices the Dhamma in accordance with the Dhamma.
If a bhikkhu is liberated by non-clinging through disenchantment, dispassion, and the cessation of ignorance, that is sufficient for him to be called a bhikkhu who has attained Nibbāna in this very life.

title: Bhikkhus, what is Right Effort? Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu generates desire for the non-arising of unarisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy,…
content: Bhikkhus, what is Right Effort? Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu generates desire for the non-arising of unarisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He generates desire for the abandoning of arisen evil, unwholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He generates desire for the arising of unarisen wholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. He generates desire for the maintenance, non-decline, increase, expansion, and fulfillment of arisen wholesome states; he makes an effort, arouses energy, applies his mind, and strives. Bhikkhus, this is called Right Effort.

title: Hemāvata: By the arising of what does the world arise? By the associating with what is the world established? By clinging to what is the…
content:
Hemāvata:
By the arising of what does the world arise?
By the associating with what is the world established?
By clinging to what is the world troubled?
By the arising of what is the world tormented?

The Blessed One:
By the arising of six the world arises,
By associating with six it is established,
By clinging to six the world is troubled,
By the arising of six the world is tormented.

title: And, Sāriputta, what is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching?" - "Here, Venerable…
content: And, Sāriputta, what is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching?" - "Here, Venerable Sir, a bhikkhu with taints destroyed lives respecting and revering the Teacher; lives respecting and revering the Dhamma; lives respecting and revering the Saṅgha; lives respecting and revering the training; lives respecting and revering concentration. This, Venerable Sir, is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching." - "Good, good, Sāriputta! This is the supreme respect that a bhikkhu with taints destroyed shows to the Tathāgata or to the Tathāgata's teaching.

title: - Householder, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. If a bhikkhu delights in…
content: - Householder, there are forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. If a bhikkhu delights in those forms, praises them, and abides with attachment to them, his consciousness becomes dependent on that and clings to it. Householder, with clinging, that bhikkhu does not attain complete peace... Householder, there are tastes cognizable by the tongue... Householder, there are mind-objects cognizable by the mind that are desirable, lovely, agreeable, pleasing, connected with sensual desire, enticing. If a bhikkhu delights in those mind-objects, praises them, and abides with attachment to them, his consciousness becomes dependent on that and clings to it. With clinging, householder, that bhikkhu does not attain Nibbāna. This, householder, is the cause, this is the condition why some beings here and now do not attain complete peace.
